books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
Food Science Lessons

books.title: The Elements of Baking
books.author: Katarina Cermelj
books.rationale: This title is ideal for learning the science of baking, in which students will learn about and observe the properties of their ingredients, experiment with substitutions, and work toward their desired product.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Gullah Geechee Home Cooking: Recipes from the Matriarch of Edisto Island
books.author: Emily Meggett
books.noteText: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing
books.maySubText: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture

books.title: Pomp and Sustenance
books.author: Mary Taylor Simeti
books.noteText: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing
books.maySubText: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture

books.title: Celtic Folklore Cooking
books.author: Joanne Asala
books.noteText: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing
books.maySubText: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture

books.title: Classic Russian Cooking
books.author: Elena Molokhovets
books.noteText: Choose this OR Mexico in Your Kitchen, Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing
books.maySubText: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture

books.title: Mexico in Your Kitchen
books.author: Mely Martínez
books.noteText: Choose this OR Gullah Geechee Home Cooking, Classic Russian Cooking, Pomp and Sustenance, Celtic Folklore Cooking, or a cookbook from another culture of your choosing
books.maySubText: a cookbook from any culture that includes both recipes and and narrative elements illustrating how food connects to the culture

books.title: Culinary Reactions: The Everyday Chemistry of Cooking
books.author: Simon Quellan Field
books.rationale: The author explains the chemistry behind a variety of culinary methods.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind, Young Reader's Edition
books.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
books.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.
books.noteText:
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

Choose this OR the standard edition

books.title: The Things We Make
books.author: Bill Hammack, Ph.D
books.rationale: From medieval cathedrals to modern soda cans, this book will help learners grasp 'the engineering method' and notice it working all around them.
books.noteText:
There is some mild sensitive content in Ch.2 for which support will be provided in lesson 1.  Teachers might choose to preview this chapter in advance.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
books.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
books.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.  This original version contains some sensitive content, which enriches the reader's understanding of the author's culture and challenges, but may not be appropriate for all learners.
books.noteText:
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

Choose this OR The Young Reader's Edition

books.title: The Way Things Work: Newly Revised Edition: The Ultimate Guide to How Things Work
books.author: David Macaulay
books.rationale: This well-written and humorously illustrated classic guides readers through the fundamental principles of machines and shows how the developments of the past are building blocks of the world of tomorrow.

books.title: By Design: Ethics, Theology, and the Practice of Engineering
books.author: Brad J. Kallenberg
books.rationale: An often overlooked topic, this text challenges students to consider the social context and formative nature of design.

books.title: Botany in a Day
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: A patterned approach to learning the plant families.

books.title: What a Plant Knows
books.author: Daniel Chamovitz
books.rationale: A plant knows more than you think! This piece of botany literature provides a fascinating look at plants.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: The Milkweed Lands: An Epic Story of One Plant: Its Nature and Ecology
books.author: Eric Lee-Mader
books.rationale: Delve into the fascinating role of this often-overlooked plant in a wide variety of ecosystems.  A collaboration between a noted ecologist and an award-winning botanical illustrator, this text will inspire readers in their own special studies.

books.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: Botany in a Day author, Thomas Elpel, produced this deck of cards to help students begin to notice the patterns that are used to characterize common plant families.
books.noteText:
For Grade 5, this resource is not required but is highly recommended for optional activities included in the lesson plans.
For the Botany Elective in HS, this resource is REQUIRED.

books.title: Growing a Revolution: Bringing Our Soil Back to Life
books.author: David Montgomery
books.rationale: Discussion of modern agriculture, the nature of soil, and how to sustainably steward the earth.
books.noteText:
For the Botany Elective in HS, this title is optional and allows the teacher to increase the literature component when appropriate.

For the Microbiology Elective in HS, choose this book OR Microbe Hunters.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: Elementary Studies in Plant Life
books.author: F.E. Fritsch & E. J. Salisbury
books.rationale: This is a hands-on look at many aspects of how plants work in a classic, digital text.  The text provides many lab activities for the course.
books.noteText: Annotations and updates to text included with lesson plans.

books.title: Tree Finder - Eastern US
books.author: May Watts
books.rationale: Regional guide
books.noteText: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

books.title: A Peterson Field Guide To Western Trees
books.author: Olivia Petrides
books.rationale: Regional guide
books.noteText: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

books.title: Pacific Coast Tree Finder
books.author: Tom Watts
books.rationale: Regional guide
books.noteText: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

books.title: Winter Tree Finder (Eastern US)
books.author: May Watts & Tom Watts
books.rationale: Regional guide
books.noteText: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

books.title: Rocky Mountain Tree Finder
books.author: Tom & Bridgett Watts
books.rationale: Regional guide
books.noteText: Choose a Tree Finder or comparable guide for your area.

books.title: The Hidden Life of Trees: The Illustrated Edition
books.author: Peter Wohlleben
books.rationale: This title provides students with current scientific information and human connections to support their knowledge about trees both as individuals and within their context.  The beautiful photography in this piece of botany literature is inspiring and supportive.

books.title: The Sibley Guide to Trees
books.author: David Allen Sibley
books.rationale: Botany students use this guide to study tree families from across North America and to consider various aspects of tree anatomy.
books.noteText: For local tree identification, students will still want one or more of the local/regional field guides.

books.title: Oaxaca Journal
books.author: Oliver Sacks
books.rationale: A master writer takes you on a trip to discover ferns in southern Mexico in this work of botany literature.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Master of Field Arts
books.author: Roseann Hanson
books.rationale: Welcome to the next level for nature journalers and field sketchers—a deep dive into becoming a dedicated master naturalist and field artist.

books.title: The Field Guide to Geology
books.author: David Lambert
books.rationale: Basic principles of geology are laid out well with helpful illustrations.

books.title: Roadside Geology/Geology Underfoot Series
books.author: various
books.rationale: Local geology guides.
books.noteText: Find your state/region, many are available on Kindle.

books.title: Annals of the Former World
books.author: John McPhee
books.rationale: Like the terrain it covers, Annals of the Former World tells a multilayered tale, and the reader may choose one of many paths through it. This is a long book, which students may or may not finish, depending on interest.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Map That Changed the World
books.author: Simon Winchester
books.rationale: A biography of a famous geologist.  This optional selection can be added to enhance the literary component of the course.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: 101 American Geo-Sites You've Gotta See
books.author: Albert Dickas
books.rationale: Geographical survey of geological sites of interest, includes a well-written geological analysis on each site and excellent coverage across regions of the U.S.  Canadian learners may prefer Aerial Geology.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.
books.maySubText: Aerial Geology by Mary Caperton Morton

books.title: The Story of the Earth in 25 Rocks
books.author: Donald R. Prothero
books.rationale: A fascinating look at what the rocks tell us according to a well-known expert in his field.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: 100 Things to See in the Night Sky
books.author: Dean Regas
books.rationale: A conversational guide through Astronomy Field Studies
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.
books.maySubText: 100 Things to See in the Southern Sky, if in the southern hemisphere.

books.title: The Crossroads of Science and Faith: Astronomy Through a Christian Worldview
books.author: Kober, Benecchi, & Gossard
books.rationale: Astronomy text with worldview discussions, which teachers may include as desired. Students that have read any Christian apologetics, such as Mere Christianity, may enjoy engaging in their own discussion and find that they do not need the additional support, however.

books.title: The Crossroads of Science and Faith: Answers to Exercises
books.author: Kober, Benecchi, & Gossard
books.rationale: Contains answers to exercises included in the accompanying text.

books.title: Astronomy Textbook
books.author: Charlesworth, Castro, & Delgado
books.rationale: This general astronomy text includes a brief history of astronomy, as well as fundamentals of the field.  It is a well-organized and concise text written from the perspective that faith and science are compatible.

books.title: The Last Stargazers
books.author: Emily Levesque
books.rationale: From the lonely quiet of midnight stargazing to tall tales of wild bears loose in the observatory, The Last Stargazers is a love letter to astronomy and an affirmation of the crucial role that humans can and must play in the future of scientific discovery.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Day Without Yesterday
books.author: John Farrell
books.rationale: An insightful story about the modern history of an evolving universe and how Georges Lemaitre convinced a generation of thinkers to embrace the notion of cosmic expansion.
books.noteText: Available in Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: Microbe Hunters
books.author: Paul De Kruif
books.rationale: Fascinating biographies of famous scientists.
books.noteText:
Choose this book OR A Planet of Viruses, Trees Up Close, & Growing a Revolution.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: A Field Guide to Bacteria
books.author: Betsy Dexter Dyer
books.rationale: A fascinating look at the world of bacteria.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: I Contain Multitudes
books.author: Ed Yong
books.rationale: Cutting-edge research on microbes.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: A Planet of Viruses
books.author: Carl Zimmer
books.rationale: This is an especially relevant book for today.
books.noteText:
Choose this book OR Microbe Hunters.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
books.author: Rebecca Skloot
books.rationale: Biography of the woman whose stolen cells shaped the development of today's medicine.
books.noteText:
Sensitive Content. 

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Micro Life
books.author: DK/Smithsonian Institution
books.rationale: An amazing close-up look at all aspects of micro life!
books.noteText: Choose this book OR Microbe Hunters

books.title: Growing a Revolution: Bringing Our Soil Back to Life
books.author: David Montgomery
books.rationale: Discussion of modern agriculture, the nature of soil, and how to sustainably steward the earth.
books.noteText:
For the Botany Elective in HS, this title is optional and allows the teacher to increase the literature component when appropriate.

For the Microbiology Elective in HS, choose this book OR Microbe Hunters.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: Illustrated Guide to Home Biology Experiments
books.author: Robert & Barbara Thompson
books.rationale: Used only for Microbiology experiments
